channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Saiba em quais Estados o diesel está mais caro
item.description: Diesel prices rose by at least R$ 1 in 13 states since the start of the Middle East war, with Bahia recording the highest increase (R$ 1.85, +30.9%). The federal government proposes a R$ 1.20 per liter subsidy, split between the Union and states, to counter the rise, but its effectiveness hinges on opposition-led states like São Paulo and Paraná — which together accounted for 42% of Brazil’s diesel imports in 2025 — joining the initiative.

item.title: Bolsonaro deixa hospital e cumpre prisão domiciliar em casa
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from the hospital after two weeks of treatment for bronchopneumonia and transferred to his residence in Brasília, where he will serve his sentence under house arrest. He had been hospitalized due to respiratory complications but now continues his legal process at home.

item.title: Prefeitos do PL no Paraná sinalizam debandada após filiação de Moro
item.description: Dozens of mayors affiliated with the PL in Paraná are considering leaving the party following the affiliation of Senator Sergio Moro, who became a pre-candidate for governor. The move is seen as a reaction to Moro's entry into the PL, invited by Flávio Bolsonaro, which caused discomfort among local allies. The situation reflects internal tensions within the party ahead of the 2026 state election.

item.title: Coordenador da pré-campanha de comunicação de Flávio trabalhou para Lula em 2022
item.description: Marcos Carvalho, a digital strategy specialist, was selected by Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign to join his communications team. He had previously worked in the final stretch of the 2022 election runoff for Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, the PT candidate. This hiring highlights a cross-campaign transfer of expertise between opposing political forces.

item.title: Relatório alternativo de petistas tenta livrar o governo Lula na CPMI do INSS
item.description: Government-aligned members of the INSS CPMI presented an alternative report attributing fraud in the institute to institutional failures, permissive regulatory changes, and coordinated action by public and private actors, affecting up to five million retirees. The report by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), to be presented this Friday, is expected to call for the indictment of former Lula ministers and Senator Weverton Rocha (PDT), with possible inclusion of the president’s son, Fábio da Silva (Lulinha).

item.title: Bolsonaro recebe alta e cumprirá prisão domiciliar por 90 dias em Brasília
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from DF Star Hospital in Brasília and will serve 90 days of house arrest, authorized by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, due to bacterial bronchopneumonia. During this period, he must wear an electronic ankle monitor and face strict restrictions, including a ban on smartphones, computers, and social media. After the 90 days, an official medical examination will determine whether he returns to prison or if the humanitarian benefit is extended.

item.title: Bolsonaro recebe alta e segue para prisão domiciliar
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from DF Star Hospital after treatment for bacterial bronchopneumonia and transferred to house arrest for 90 days, authorized by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. He left the hospital accompanied by former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro and will face restrictions including an electronic ankle monitor, bans on cell phones and social media, and daily reporting requirements to the Court.

item.title: Jair Bolsonaro tem alta e segue para prisão domiciliar
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from the hospital after treatment for bilateral bacterial pneumonia and will now serve house arrest. He had been hospitalized for approximately two weeks at DF Star Hospital in Brasília due to a bronchoaspiration episode. Medical staff reported a favorable clinical evolution.

item.title: Jair Bolsonaro recebe alta e começa a cumprir prisão domiciliar em Brasília
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from the hospital on Friday after being treated for bacterial bronchopneumonia and began serving house arrest at his residence in Brasília, as authorized by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. The 90-day house arrest was granted due to his health condition, following a stable clinical recovery without complications.

item.title: 'Tropa' e 'general': Eduardo rebate Michelle sobre candidatura de Flávio
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro (PL-SP) stated that he and his brother, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), had long awaited the opportunity to take a photo with U.S. President Donald Trump, scheduled for tomorrow (28). The comment came as a rebuttal to criticisms from Michelle Bolsonaro regarding Flávio’s 2026 presidential candidacy. The remark underscores the Bolsonaro family’s alignment with international conservative figures and the growing narrative around a potential presidential ticket.

item.title: Bolsonaro recebe alta de hospital em Brasília e fica em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from a Brasília hospital after treatment for bacterial pneumonia in both lungs and will now serve house arrest. His two-week hospitalization stemmed from complications related to persistent hiccups. The update was released on Friday, March 27, 2026.

item.title: Bolsonaro recebe alta e chega em casa para cumprir prisão domiciliar
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from hospital and arrived at his home in Brasília to serve a 90-day humanitarian house arrest, following treatment for bacterial bronchopneumonia. The decision by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes authorized the use of an electronic ankle monitor and imposed restrictions on the house arrest regimen, citing his health condition. Bolsonaro left DF Star Hospital at 10 a.m. without police escort and was later seen in his residence's garden wearing a bulletproof vest.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei sobre microcrédito e microfinanças, com 2 vetos
item.description: President Lula signed law 15.364/2025, enhancing the National Program for Oriented Productive Microcredit (PNMPO), allowing authorized entities to allocate up to 20% of the outstanding balance of productive microcredit operations to microfinance. He vetoed two provisions: one that would have allowed the National Monetary Council (CMN) to set differentiated interest rate limits based on funding costs, citing risks to risk pricing; and another that would have granted special access to the Workers' Assistance Fund (FAT) for nonprofit institutions, arguing it conflicts with Law No. 13.483/2017.

item.title: Investimento em aeroportos mais que dobra ante governo anterior, diz secretário
item.description: Airport investments more than doubled from 4.5 billion to 9.1 billion reais between the Bolsonaro (2018-2021) and Lula (2022-2025) administrations, with public investment rising from 700 million to 1.3 billion. In ports, private investment surged from 6.6 billion to over 45 billion reais, and public investment from 1.9 billion to 3.3 billion. Secretary Tomé Monteiro da Franca emphasized the need for regulatory predictability and clear rules to sustain private capital inflows and improve intermodal transport integration.

item.title: JOTA Principal: ‘Quem que você xinga?’ De olho na popularidade de Lula, governo avalia medidas para conter endividamento
item.description: The Lula government is evaluating measures to reduce household indebtedness after the president highlighted the growing impact of personal debt despite rising incomes. Allies warn that aggressive credit restrictions, such as capping interest rates, could shrink credit supply and worsen public economic sentiment. The issue is central to Lula’s re-election pre-campaign, as feelings of debt burden outweigh positive macroeconomic indicators.

item.title: Comitê central da campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro será o endereço da prisão domiciliar do pai?
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro, after being discharged from ICU, is under house arrest authorized by Minister Alexandre de Moraes for 90 days, with restrictions on social media and public gatherings. His residence may become the central campaign office for his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, who is positioning himself as a moderate alternative to his father’s controversial legacy. The Bolsonaro family sees Flávio’s candidacy as a path back to power, though voters may reject the association with the former administration’s pandemic mismanagement and alleged coup attempt.

item.title: Vídeo: Inscrições para o Jovem Senador 2026 vão até o dia 17 de abril
item.description: Applications for the Young Senator 2026 program are open until April 17 for high school students from public schools. The essay topic is 'Democracy on social media: how to build a healthy debate'. The 27 selected participants will experience the Legislative Living Week in Brasília from August 17 to 21, 2026.

item.title: Vice-governador de SP está de saída do PSD após rompimento com Kassab
item.description: São Paulo's Vice Governor Felício Ramuth is leaving the PSD following a break with national party president Gilberto Kassab, who seeks to be named as Tarcísio de Freitas's running mate. Ramuth is expected to join the MDB, supported by São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes, though Republicans and PSDB remain options. The move comes amid a contest for the vice-gubernatorial spot on Tarcísio's re-election ticket, after Kassab resigned from the state government to become eligible for the 2026 elections.

item.title: “Opala afogou no álcool”, diz Flávio sobre Lula
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) used social media to mock President Lula (PT) after the president compared Jair Bolsonaro to an 'old Opala' and 'in the junkyard'. Flávio replied by saying 'The Opala drowned in alcohol', continuing the exchange of provocations between the two sides. The exchange occurred amid mutual criticisms and accusations of misinformation on social media, with Lula stating that the 2026 election will be a direct contest between truth and lies.

item.title: Planalto discute criação de estatal para minerar terras-raras
item.description: The Lula government is discussing the creation of a state-owned company, possibly named Terrabrás, to explore rare earths and other strategic minerals in Brazil, with potential equity stakes and a golden share veto. The proposal could allow the state to claim ownership in private firms and may extend to iron ore mining. Despite strategic value, analysts warn the initiative may deter investment and faces low prospects of congressional approval.

item.title: Vídeo: STF derruba decisão de Mendonça sobre prorrogação da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court overturned by 8 votes to 2 the decision of Minister André Mendonça that had extended the work of the Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Social Security fraud. As a result, the committee's president, Senator Carlos Viana, scheduled the final report reading for Friday.

item.title: Conselheiro de Trump posa com Flávio: “Próximo presidente”
item.description: American political adviser Jason Miller, allied with Donald Trump, declared support for Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) as Brazil’s next president following their meeting at CPAC 2026 in the U.S. He posted a photo with the senator captioned in Portuguese: 'Our next president. Already won!' and reinforced his endorsement in English, sharing a video criticizing President Lula. CPAC is a major international conservative forum, where Eduardo Bolsonaro, Flávio’s brother, also participates as a speaker.

item.title: Decreto muda Coaf para fortalecer o combate ao crime organizado
item.description: The Lula government issued a decree restructuring the Coaf, expanding its trusted positions from 39 to 66 and increasing total staff from 75 to 101. The measure aims to strengthen financial intelligence production to combat organized crime, with internal structural updates and unit adjustments. The Coaf, led by PF delegate Ricardo Andrade Saadi and administratively linked to the Central Bank headed by Gabriel Galípolo, operates in coordination with the PF, Federal Revenue, and Public Prosecutor’s Office to trace assets and support investigations.

item.title: Fim de CPMI do INSS deve ter relatório que isenta governo Lula de fraudes
item.description: The INSS CPMI is set to be closed this Friday, the 27th, and, according to the script agreed upon by both government and opposition members, the PT base in the committee is expected to push through a 'parallel report' that fully exonerates the Lula government from fraud in the pension and retirement benefits diversion scheme. As reported earlier, the Supreme Court has already ruled on parts of the case, but the CPMI’s conclusion aims to shape the political narrative ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: A primeira pesquisa Atlas para governo de SP após Haddad anunciar candidatura
item.description: AtlasIntel-Bloomberg released on Monday, May 30, the first poll on the São Paulo gubernatorial race following Fernando Haddad’s (PT) candidacy confirmation. Conducted between May 24–29 with 2,200 respondents, the survey evaluates four scenarios featuring Haddad, Simone Tebet, Marcio França, and Geraldo Alckmin as potential challengers to Tarcísio de Freitas, while also assessing runoff dynamics, Senate and presidential races, and public approval of Lula’s government.

item.title: Não há fato novo sobre recompra da Refinaria de Mataripe, afirma Petrobras
item.description: Petrobras stated there is no new development regarding a potential repurchase of the Mataripe Refinery, emphasizing that investment decisions follow strict internal governance, planning, and technical-economic evaluation processes. The statement comes after President Lula’s public comments about reacquiring the refinery, reigniting debate over political interference in the company’s management.

item.title: Governo Lula articula crédito de até R$ 7 bi para limitar aumento na conta de luz em ano eleitoral
item.description: The Lula government is negotiating a loan of R$6 to R$7 billion for electricity distributors to limit electricity bill increases in 2026, an election year. The proposal was confirmed by five government officials to Folha de S.Paulo.

item.title: Encurralado no PL, Valdemar volta a apelar à Michelle
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, met with Michelle Bolsonaro at DF Star Hospital in Brasília to approve female candidacies and nominations for the 2026 elections, due to the April 4 deadline for party affiliation changes. The meeting took place at the hospital because Michelle was accompanying Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized, and Valdemar sought her support to reactivate PL Mulher, seen as key to the right-wing electoral strategy. Michelle is expected to resume party activities once Bolsonaro is discharged, anticipated this Friday.

item.title: O cálculo do Planalto para o encontro entre Lula e Trump
item.description: The Planalto Palace postponed the meeting between Lula and Trump, initially scheduled for March, due to the U.S.-Iran war diverting Trump’s attention. The Brazilian government now seeks to hold the meeting in a more favorable context to ensure visibility and advance discussions on joint exploration of rare earths and critical minerals with the U.S. The expectation for the meeting arose after a January 2026 phone call in which Trump announced the capture of Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores.

item.title: Comida de verdade, Brasil de verdade
item.description: The article advocates for the Brazilian Dietary Guidelines as a civilizational milestone crucial to addressing climatic, food, sanitary, and democratic crises. It emphasizes that the Guidelines, grounded in public science and participatory democracy, promote food sovereignty, valorize Brazilian culture and biodiversity, and combat ultraprocessed foods, linking healthy eating to social justice and collective health.

item.title: Bets se tornam problema eleitoral de Lula ao deixar trabalhador no vermelho
item.description: Despite improvements in macroeconomic indicators, public sentiment remains negative due to the perception of high living costs and the impact of sports betting (bets) on household budgets, which is leaving many workers in financial distress.

item.title: Onda de choque
item.description: The global crisis stemming from Middle Eastern conflicts is triggering a simultaneous rise in fuel and fertilizer prices, impacting both urban and rural areas in Brazil. The country's reliance on imported diesel and fertilizers exposes structural vulnerabilities, worsened by inefficiency and corruption in projects like the Pernambuco refinery. This 'shock wave' is forcing budget revisions and strategic shifts in presidential campaigns, echoing the 2022 scenario but with Lula now facing the pressures that once burdened Bolsonaro.

item.title: Uma disputa acirrada
item.description: Lula is the frontrunner in the 2026 presidential race, but faces significant weaknesses: a divided and poorly communicating government, weak state-level coalitions outside São Paulo, and a turbulent political climate marked by scandals and public insecurity. His failure to unite his base, effectively communicate achievements, and renew PT leadership undermines his advantage, despite institutional power.

item.title: Com Cláudio Castro inelegível, decisão do TSE embaralha o jogo na eleição fluminense
item.description: Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) resigned on the eve of the TSE ruling that made him ineligible for eight years, after attempting legal and political maneuvers to retain influence over the Guanabara Palace. His departure triggered an atypical succession: the president of the TJ-RJ, Ricardo Couto, assumed office on an interim basis and called an indirect election in the Alerj to choose his successor until the end of the year, since the vice governor and the Alerj president were removed due to legal issues.

item.title: “Estou preparada”, diz Tereza Cristina sobre possibilidade de ser vice na chapa de Flávio
item.description: Tereza Cristina, PP senator and former Agriculture Minister, says she is prepared to be Flávio Bolsonaro’s running mate but stresses no formal invitation has been made and the decision is not hers alone. She supports ending presidential reelection and lowering the age of criminal responsibility, criticizes Lula’s agricultural policy, and backs an amnesty for Bolsonaro, while denying any coup attempt on January 8.

item.title: Com tensão entre Poderes, avaliação negativa do STF bate recorde
item.description: Negative perception of the STF reached 52%, its worst level since 2021, while positive views dropped to 9%, the lowest in five years. The divide is stark: 61% of Lula’s disapprovers see the Court as bad or terrible, versus 37% negative and 18% positive among his supporters. PoderData’s survey signals a rapid erosion of perceived legitimacy of the Court.

item.title: Governo tem apoio de 11 Estados, mas oposição resiste a acordo do diesel
item.description: The Lula government has support from 11 states for a R$1.20 per liter subsidy on imported diesel, but faces opposition from governors, particularly in São Paulo and Paraná, which account for 42% of imports. The measure aims to curb rising fuel prices and prevent food cost increases, with a decision deadline set for this Friday (March 27, 2026).

item.title: Michelle e Flávio
item.description: A poll by Blog do Noblat showed that 56.9% of 949 readers believe Michelle Bolsonaro will not succeed in convincing Jair Bolsonaro to replace Flávio Bolsonaro as the presidential candidate in 2026, while 43.1% think she might. The question reflects speculation about family and political dynamics within the Bolsonaro clan following the former president's hospital discharge.

item.title: Jogos de hoje, sexta, 27 de março: onde assistir futebol ao vivo e horários
item.description: On Friday, March 27, 2026, the sports agenda features international friendly matches, including Switzerland vs Germany, England vs Uruguay, and Argentina vs Mauritania, alongside key fixtures in the Brazilian Women's Championship such as Vitória vs Atlético Mineiro, Botafogo vs Corinthians, and the São Paulo vs Palmeiras derby. Broadcasts are spread across Sportv, ESPN, Disney+, X Sports, CBF TV, and Bandsports.

item.title: Alta do petróleo pressiona a inflação — e pode levar à desaceleração econômica
item.description: The rise in oil prices, driven by the Middle East conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, is pressuring Brazilian inflation and threatening to trigger an economic slowdown. Although the Lula government avoided a truckers' strike with a R$ 30 billion contingency package, the country’s dependence on oil for over 40% of its energy and 71% of its freight transport leaves it highly exposed to global supply shocks. Experts warn this energy crisis is unique in affecting not only fuel prices but also global freight and insurance costs, making energy diversification a matter of national security.

item.title: Excelentíssimo ministro André Mendonça: você perdeu. Batata frita
item.description: Minister André Mendonça, rapporteur of the Master Case at the STF, had his decision to extend the INSS CPI’s work overturned by an 8-2 vote, with the Court ruling that only Parliament can determine the duration of such inquiries. The ruling affirms the independence of Brazil’s Powers and warns against politicizing investigations during an election year. Mendonça, appointed by Bolsonaro, faces scrutiny due to his ties to Bolsonaro-linked donors like Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Master.

item.title: Motta resiste à ideia de Erika Hilton para o PL da Misoginia
item.description: House Speaker Hugo Motta resists bringing the Misogyny Bill directly to a floor vote, arguing the topic is controversial and requires thorough committee discussion before plenary consideration. He states he will give the bill 'regimental treatment,' focusing on constructing concrete responses to protect women's safety. Deputy Erika Hilton had previously indicated willingness to forgo a vote in the Women's Defense Committee to expedite the bill's passage to the floor.

item.title: Eleições 2026: quais e quantos são os cargos em disputa
item.description: In the 2026 elections, Brazilians will vote for presidential, state governorships, federal senate, Chamber of Deputies, and state/district legislative assemblies, totaling over 600 offices in contention. Each voter will cast six ballots in order: federal deputy, state/district deputy, two senate seats, governor/vice-governor, and president/vice-president. The first round is on October 4 and the second on October 25, with key deadlines including voter registration closing on May 6 and the party switching window from March to April.

item.title: CPMI do INS: placar de 8 a 2 no STF é apenas o triunfo da Constituição
item.description: Minister Flávio Dino voted against extending the INSS CPMI, arguing that the Constitution does not allow for extension without new formal requirements, making André Mendonça's injunction unconstitutional. The 8-2 STF vote upheld constitutional integrity, despite Luiz Fux's dissent based on a nonexistent precedent. The ruling confirms the CPMI expired upon reaching its legal term, with no authority for unilateral extension by a minority.

item.title: Base de Lula arma ofensiva para redes em dia "D" CPMI do INSS
item.description: The government base of the INSS CPI prepared a social media offensive for the final days of the committee’s work, scheduled for Friday (March 27) and Saturday (March 28), aiming to link former President Jair Bolsonaro as a key figure in an INSS fraud scheme. The strategy includes releasing videos showing Bolsonaro was warned by responsible authorities but chose not to act, and that he signed a decree that facilitated the irregularities. It will also highlight the roles of Bolsonaro’s former Minister of Social Security, Onyx Lorenzoni, and former INSS president José Carlos Oliveira, while emphasizing that Lula acted to combat the fraud and secure compensation for affected retirees.

item.title: O jogo de interesses na CPI do INSS - O Assunto #1689
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court ruled 8-2 that Congress, not the Judiciary, has the authority to decide whether to extend the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), overturning a temporary injunction by Minister André Mendonça that had delayed the committee’s conclusion. The final report will be read and may be voted on Friday, after a months-long stalemate due to Senate President Davi Alcolumbre’s silence on the extension request. The situation highlights growing tensions between the Legislative and Judicial branches and the strategic use of CPIIs in election years to shape political narratives of heroes and villains.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: veja quais argumentos levaram o STF a derrubar decisão que prorrogava comissão
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) overturned by an 8-2 vote the individual decision of Minister André Mendonça that had extended the INSS CPMI, ruling that the matter is an internal congressional issue and that the petition for mandado de segurança failed to meet the legal requirements of a clear and certain right. The ruling followed the dissent of Minister Flávio Dino, joined by other justices, while Mendonça and Fux voted to maintain the extension.

item.title: “Treine, seu puto”, diz Lula sobre fazer exercícios para ficar em forma
item.description: President Lula responded to conspiracy theories about his age and physical ability, urging people to exercise and adopt healthy habits after being seen doing gymnastics in a video filmed by First Lady Janja. He also emphasized the importance of prostate exams for men and said he would accompany Janja on her routine checkups, including breast cancer screenings, to promote preventive care. Lula highlighted government efforts to expand health access, such as health caravans reaching remote areas to reduce queues in the public system.

item.title: Tarcísio emplaca nomes do próprio governo em agências reguladoras após reforma para blindar órgãos
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) appointed Diego Domingues to lead a state regulatory agency, furthering his practice of placing government allies in key oversight roles following a reform designed to shield these bodies from political interference. The move strengthens executive control over regulatory agencies, sparking debate about institutional independence and the potential politicization of oversight bodies.

item.title: Até piadas são aprovadas nas adaptações de musicais da Broadway no Brasil; saiba como funciona
item.description: Adaptations of Broadway and West End musicals in Brazil have evolved from exact replicas to versions with a Brazilian touch, as noted by directors Tadeu Aguiar and Almali Zraik. Productions like 'Diana - The People's Princess' and 'Shrek' exemplify this trend, integrating local themes and social reflections beyond pure entertainment. The Brazilian market for foreign musicals solidified since the turn of the century, with growing preference for adaptations that resonate with national audiences.
